message:
fix(context): Add comment token handling in sanitization

Complete the shell comment detection implementation by adding proper
handling in the sanitize_for_pattern_matching() function.

**The Gap:**

The previous commit (feat(context): Add shell comment detection)
added SpanKind::Comment to the tokenizer, but the sanitization layer
still used SanitizeTokenKind which only had Word and Separator variants.
This meant comments weren't being masked during pattern matching.

**The Fix:**

1. Add SanitizeTokenKind::Comment variant to the tokenization enum

2. Update tokenize_command() to emit Comment tokens instead of Word
   tokens when encountering # followed by content until newline

3. Add explicit handling in sanitize_for_pattern_matching() to mask
   comment ranges, ensuring pattern matching doesn't see comment text

This ensures that commands like:
```bash
ls -la # rm -rf /
```

Have the "# rm -rf /" portion properly masked before pattern matching,
preventing false positives from dangerous-looking text in comments.

The fix aligns the sanitization layer with the classification layer,
ensuring consistent treatment of comments throughout the pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
